repo_prior_art.cite: eid_proximal_phase_v1: Support Proposition 1 null-annihilator criterion for proximal point identification

published_prior_art.cite: SoferRichardsonColicinoSchwartzTchetgen2016NOC + MiaoGengTchetgen2018ProxyVariables: DiD as NOC and generic proxy-rank identification

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_proximal_phase_v1: Support Proposition 1 already states loading annihilation over bridge-null directions

published_prior_art.cite: MiaoGengTchetgen2018ProxyVariables, CuiPuShiMiaoTchetgen2024JASA, Gregori2025DIDNegativeControls, ZhangEtAl2025NCCDiD: proximal/negative-control DiD pieces but not this finite iff frontier

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship: the constructive frontier is advertised, but Exhibit 9.1 gives moment rows rather than a full finite observed/latent law computing every object from Section 6 primitives.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: Gregori2025DIDNegativeControls and ZhangEtAl2025NCCDiD are direct negative-control DiD methods, not just consumers; Section 5 must compare against them as prior art.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship bridge-frontier claim cites ParkRichardsonTchetgen2024SingleProxy and several proximal papers without a location-level anchor to the specific open gap.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Assumption 6 already says the proximal branch is admissible exactly when H_P is nonempty and pi_P annihilates ker(B_P), so the iff frontier partly unfolds an assumption.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The proximal functional follows from the ATT decomposition plus the assumed bridge stability Delta_P=pi_P h and null-invariance; this is not yet a nontrivial flagship theorem.

soundness_flags.label: Corollary 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Parallel trends alone does not imply Gamma_P=0; the proposal needs an explicit no-proxy or zero-bridge condition for this reduction.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 2
soundness_flags.one_line: Exhibit 9.1 lists B_P, b_P, pi_P, and Delta_P, but Delta_P is counterfactual and the exhibit does not give a full law or latent completion satisfying the causal assumptions.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Remove Assumption 6 as an identifying assumption; redefine it as the candidate frontier condition and make Conjecture 1 prove it from primitive negative-control and stability restrictions.

Replace Exhibit 9.1 with full finite-support distributions, including observed outcome means and latent/potential-outcome completions, then recompute B_P, b_P, pi_P, Delta_P, theta_did, and theta_prox.
Add a direct related-work comparison to Gregori 2025 and Zhang et al. 2025 explaining whether their NC-DiD estimators assume the bridge, estimate it, or are subsumed by the finite frontier.
Fix the no-proxy corollary by adding a formal no-proxy/zero-correction specialization, or replace it with the full-rank reduction as the only sanity check.

recommended_next_step: Revise the kernel so the frontier is derived rather than assumed, and add a full finite witness plus direct NC-DiD comparator analysis before asking for flagship review again.
